title: Contrato de arrendamiento — Spanish month-to-month rental template
html:
Contrato de arrendamiento — Spanish month-to-month rental template

Source: Spanish-language residential lease template Yahir drafted on 2025-09-13. Mes-por-mes lease, 15-day notice, cash-only rent, $X security deposit, no smoking/pets/mail/lock-changes/exterior-mechanics, 24-hr landlord-entry notice, 3-day max guest stays, 10pm noise rule.

TL;DR

  • Form-template, not a signed contract — names, addresses, dates, dollar amounts all blank-lined. Yahir was building a reusable Spanish lease for someone else's use, not signing one himself.
  • Mes-por-mes structure — month-to-month with a 15-day written termination notice on either side, 5-day grace period after due date.
  • Strict house rules — no smoking, no pets, no mail to address, no lock changes without consent, one parking space, no exterior cooking or auto-repair, guests max 3 consecutive days, 10pm quiet hours.
  • Cash-only payments — "Forma de Pago: Todos los pagos de la renta deberán realizarse en efectivo" — informal-economy / cash-economy signal.
  • Companion to the IDA translations doc — both are Spanish-language documents Yahir produced for non-English-fluent community members, three months apart.

Pattern observed

This sits inside the Spanish-language-as-service thread that the December IDA translations later extends — Yahir using his bilingual capacity to produce administrative documents (lease, IDA explainer) for the community. The cash-only + no-mail-to-address rules suggest the template was drafted for a specific landlord-tenant relationship in mind, possibly within Yahir's family or extended network, where the Austin-era informal housing economy intersects his bilingual administrative-help mode.

Open questions

  • Whose lease was this? (Family member's? A Gtano's-era contact in Grants Pass? An Austin-era housing arrangement?)
  • Did the lease actually get signed and used?

Related

  • 2025 12 05 ida translations — the December IDA translations document, same Spanish-as-service mode
  • ida individual development account
  • austin tx
